rows.finalText: "How do I commit to the path that feels deeply aligned" is best read here through Purpose / Calling with a commitment emphasis. One clear focal point is Querent (29) in Scythe House. It brings your own stance toward the path, including how much of it is truly chosen and how much is inherited habit to the foreground, and the rest of the spread suggests that your role and boundaries set the tone. In lived terms, this is a vocation question where meaning, sustainability, and the right path are not identical things. The harder part is your stance toward the path moving through decisive cuts, but there is still room for a small but real opening around resources and flow. Taken together, this is not a closed field; it is a live situation where your stance toward the path moving through decisive cuts has to be named before a small but real opening around resources and flow can fully develop. The future stays more open with a small but real opening around resources and flow, so long as your stance toward the path moving through decisive cuts does not take over. Start in Scythe House: stop feeding the version of the path that only survives by urgency, guilt, or habit, because that is where the strain is clearest.

rows.finalText: On "What is the next right step in my calling", this 3-card leans into purpose / calling themes with a right path emphasis. Crossroads (22) in Past sets the tone with decision quality now shapes next cycle.
